narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Cheap and abundant oil and natural gas have to go, Gore says – for poor people, anyway – in order for the planet to stop warming.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore's stance on climate policies, specifically his call to eliminate cheap and abundant oil and natural gas, implying that this would harm poor people.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent figure in the climate movement, for his perceived hypocrisy in using large amounts of fossil fuels despite advocating for their reduction.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by pointing out the supposed failure of past predictions and implying that current warnings are also exaggerated.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the accuracy of climate science by highlighting the alleged failure of past predictions, implying that the science is unreliable or flawed.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: “rain bombs” and “boiling oceans”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement for using sensational and misleading language to describe weather events, implying that the movement is driven by hysteria rather than facts.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: The legislation contains $369 billion, not including tax credits, in appropriation funds for “renewable energy,” meaning windmills, solar panels, and a very dark and cold future for humanity.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text expresses skepticism about the benefits of renewable energy technologies, implying that they are not a viable or desirable solution to energy needs.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Unless America and all other sovereign nations open up their borders to an illegal alien onslaught, the climate crisis will only continue to worsen, Gore went on to allege.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text implies that there is a hidden plot to use the climate crisis as a pretext for opening borders and allowing mass immigration, suggesting that powerful groups are manipulating the climate narrative for their own purposes.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Cheap and abundant oil and natural gas have to go, Gore says – for poor people, anyway – in order for the planet to stop warming.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes Gore's climate policy by implying it unfairly targets poor people and ignores the benefits of cheap and abundant energy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote challenges Gore's integrity and credibility on climate issues by highlighting his personal use of fossil fuels, implying hypocrisy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote downplays the significance of climate change by suggesting that past predictions have been incorrect and exaggerated, implying that current warnings are also unfounded.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote raises doubts about the scientific consensus on climate change by pointing out the alleged failure of past predictions, implying that the science is flawed or unreliable.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: “rain bombs” and “boiling oceans”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes the climate movement by using the term 'cultists' and implying that their language is exaggerated and scare-mongering.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: The legislation contains $369 billion, not including tax credits, in appropriation funds for “renewable energy,” meaning windmills, solar panels, and a very dark and cold future for humanity.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote expresses skepticism about green technologies by implying that they are not viable or desirable, and that investing in them will lead to a negative outcome.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Unless America and all other sovereign nations open up their borders to an illegal alien onslaught, the climate crisis will only continue to worsen, Gore went on to allege.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote implies that there is a hidden plot or agenda behind Gore's climate warnings, suggesting that he is using the issue to push for open borders and undermine national sovereignty.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: "rain bombs" and "boiling oceans"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement by using the term 'climate cultists' and implying that their language is 'bizarre' and scare-mongering, thus challenging the motives and integrity of the movement.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by stating that past predictions have not come true, implying that current warnings are also unlikely to be accurate.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the scientific consensus on climate change by highlighting the supposed failure of past predictions, thus raising doubts about the accuracy of climate science.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent figure in the climate movement, by implying that he is hypocritical in his use of fossil fuels, thus challenging his integrity and the credibility of the movement.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: The legislation contains $369 billion, not including tax credits, in appropriation funds for "renewable energy," meaning windmills, solar panels, and a very dark and cold future for humanity.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text expresses skepticism about green technologies by implying that they will lead to a 'dark and cold future for humanity', thus criticizing the potential consequences of transitioning to renewable energy.
